diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 9d6ef3a..839427e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,54 +1,47 @@
-# NovaMind Strategy — Week 5 (Day 35)
+# NovaMind Strategy — Week 6 (Day 42)
 
 ## Current State
-- Cash: $612,444
-- 446 subscribers: S1: 244 Plan A + 197 Plan B, S2: 3 Plan B, S3: 2 Plan B
-- R&D Tier 1: COMPLETED! +0.0289 quality boost
-- R&D Tier 2: STARTED! Cost $333K, expected Day 65 completion, +0.062 quality
-- 43 open issues (42 S1, 1 S2, avg age 0 days)
-- No outages, no overload. Usage 91K / 200K cap (45.6%)
+- Cash: $229,646 (dropped $383K — R&D Tier 2 cost $333K + ~$50K burn)
+- 689 subscribers: S1: 671 (394A+276B+1C), S2: 7 (1A+6B), S3: 11 (1A+10B)
+- R&D Tier 1: COMPLETED (+0.0289 quality)
+- R&D Tier 2: IN PROGRESS (Day 35 start, expected ~Day 52, +0.07 mean quality)
+- 36 open issues (S1), 1 open issue (S3), all new (age 0)
+- OUTAGE: 90 min this week — 207K usage exceeded Tier 1 200K capacity
 
-## Key Metrics
-- Weekly burn: ~$46.5K (dev=$36.5K, ops=$6.2K, ads=$3.3K, capacity+compute=$1.6K, lead_acq=$0.2K)
-- Revenue: $1,729/week ($216/day) — growing with subscriber count
-- Monthly recurring: ~$4,250 (244×$5 + 202×$15)
-- S1 conversion strong — dominating subscriber base
-- S2: 3 subs (struggling — q_min 0.37 vs quality 0.28 on Plan B)
-- S3: 2 subs (growing slowly — quality 0.34 vs q_min 0.145)
-- D_E09: 0 subs (quality 0.27 vs q_min 0.199 — close)
+## Key Metrics (After Actions)
+- Daily burn: ~$6,370 (capacity=$530, ops=$1,650, dev=$3,300, ads=$650, compute~$200, leads~$40)
+- Weekly burn: ~$44,590
+- Revenue: ~$328/day, ~$2,296/week
+- Net weekly: -$42,294
+- Runway: ~5.2 weeks at current burn
+- Monthly recurring: ~$9,835 (S1: $9,495, S2: $130, S3: $210)
 
 ## Actions Taken This Week
-1. **Price increases**: A $5→$8, B $15→$20, C $30→$35 (S1 has huge quality headroom)
-2. **Quota bumps**: A 500→750, B 1200→1800, C 3000→4500 (addressing #1 complaint)
-3. **Ops**: $800→$1000/day + $100/day S1 targeted (43 issues)
-4. **Targeted dev restructured**: S2 $800, S3 $1000, D_E09 $500/day (dropped S1 — unnecessary)
-5. **R&D Tier 2 started**: $333K cost, expected Day 65 (+0.062 quality)
-6. **Lead promos**: S3 $5/mo, S2 $3/mo off first billing to boost conversion
-7. **Ad spend**: $650/day across channels (S1 content+referral, S3 content+social+search, S2 content+referral, D_E09 search+linkedin)
-8. **Social post**: Announced quota increases and smarter throttling
+1. **Capacity: Tier 1→2** ($530/day, 800K units) — outage at 103% forced immediate upgrade
+2. **Global ops: $1000→$1500/day** + $150 S1 targeted — 37 issues, social media support complaints
+3. **Global dev: reduced $3000→$2000/day** — diminishing log returns, need cash conservation
+4. **Targeted dev: S2 $800, S3 $300, D_E09 $200/day** (reduced from S3 $1000→$300, D_E09 $500→$200)
+5. **Price: Plan A $8→$10** (S1 quality 0.32 vs q_min 0.08 — massive headroom)
+6. **Social post**: Addressed support complaints, announced capacity 4x upgrade
 
 ## Strategy
-- **S1 is the cash cow**: 441 subs, massive quality headroom. Price increase from $5→$8 is safe. Quota bump keeps satisfaction up.
-- **S2 needs more dev love**: q_min 0.37 is tough. Increased targeted dev from $300→$800/day. R&D Tier 2 will help. Quality needs to hit ~0.40+ on Plan B.
-- **S3 growing**: Quality 0.34 vs q_min 0.145 — healthy margin. $5 lead promo to accelerate conversion.
-- **D_E09 is the first enterprise target**: q_min 0.199, our quality 0.272 — above threshold. $500/day targeted dev to pull ahead. $100/day ads.
-- **Enterprise (E1-E3)**: Still quality-gated — need R&D Tier 2+3 before they're reachable.
-- **Runway**: $612K at $51.5K/week burn = ~12 weeks runway. Need revenue to grow faster than costs.
+- **Cash conservation is priority #1**: $229K at $44.5K/week burn = 5 weeks. R&D Tier 2 (~$333K spent) must deliver.
+- **S1 is 97% of revenue**: 671 subs. Price increase $8→$10 is safe given 0.32 quality vs 0.08 q_min. Need to monitor churn at first billing cycle with new price.
+- **S2 struggling**: Only 7 subs. q_min 0.37 vs quality 0.317 on Plan B — still below threshold. R&D Tier 2 (+0.07) will push quality to ~0.39, crossing the line.
+- **S3 growing slowly**: 11 subs, quality 0.385 vs q_min 0.145 — healthy. Reduced dev spend since headroom is big.
+- **D_E09**: Close — quality 0.302 vs q_min 0.199. Need to trigger enterprise negotiation threads.
+- **Enterprise (E1-E3)**: Still far — need q_min 0.67-1.09. R&D Tier 3+ needed. Months away.
 
 ## Watch List
-- **Price increase impact**: Watch for conversion drop on Plan A especially
-- **Churn at first billing**: Price-sensitive S1 users hitting Day 30+ billing at $8 instead of $5
-- **S2 conversion improvement**: $800/day targeted dev should accelerate quality toward 0.37 threshold
-- **Issue resolution speed**: 43 open issues with $1000 ops + $100 S1 targeted
-- **Usage growth**: 91K/200K (45.6%). At current growth (2x every 2 weeks), Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K) needed ~Week 8-9
-- **R&D Tier 2 completion**: Day 65 (+0.062 quality) — big unlock for S2 and enterprise
+- **S1 churn at $10 billing**: 394 Plan A users will see $10 charge instead of $5 from 2 weeks ago
+- **R&D Tier 2 completion ~Day 52**: +0.07 quality boost is critical for S2 and D_E09
+- **Cash: dropping below $200K triggers emergency mode**
+- **Issue resolution speed**: 37 open issues with $1,650/day ops
+- **Usage at Tier 2 (800K capacity)**: 207K currently, growing ~30%/week → 800K reached ~Week 11
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- **Outage at 98.7% capacity**: Don't wait for 100%. Upgrade at ~80% utilization.
-- **Targeted dev spend works cumulatively**: S3 went from 0→2 subs. Group-specific quality bonus accumulates and persists.
-- **Social media complaints are leading indicators**: Rate limit complaints preceded the outage. Quota complaints signal pricing/quality misalignment.
-- **Revenue is tiny vs burn**: $1,729/week vs $46.5K burn. 27:1 ratio. Need to grow revenue 10x just to get to 3:1.
-- **Price increases on S1 are safe**: Quality 0.25-0.30 vs q_min 0.08 gives huge headroom for $5→$8.
-- **Compute costs are small**: $54/day vs $7K+ fixed costs. Usage-based pricing is dominated by fixed dev/ops/ad spend.
-- **Don't waste dev spend on groups with massive quality headroom**: S1 had +0.037 group bonus but didn't need it. Redirected to S2/S3/D_E09.
-- **R&D Tier 1 completed at +0.029 (mean was 0.04)**: Slightly below average result. Tier 2 expected +0.062.
\ No newline at end of file
+- **Never let usage exceed 80% capacity**: 207K > 200K → outage. Tier 1 caps at 200K. Should have upgraded at ~160K.
+- **R&D Tier 2 cost $333K was 54% of cash**: Too aggressive. Should have conserved cash for runway.
+- **Dev spending has log-saturation**: Global dev $3000→$2000 won't hurt much. Need R&D for quality jumps.
+- **Support complaints spike hard after outages**: Social media full of support complaints today. Ops spending must lead, not lag.
+- **Revenue per S1 sub is tiny vs costs**: $8-20/mo vs $65/mo fully-loaded cost. Need enterprise deals to cross the chasm.
\ No newline at end of file
